title: vera-duthiea macrocarpa
intro: Vera-duthiea macrocarpa, also known as the Papuan warpaint tree or Papuan war-paint tree, is a flowering plant species native to New Guinea. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly the bark, which has been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Vera-duthiea macrocarpa include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce swelling, fight infections, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the bark is often dried and powdered, then used to make teas, infusions, or topical creams, although its use should be carefully considered and discussed with a healthcare professional.
